description_long: This sand patch consists of a roughly rectangular section of compacted, brownish sand with a slightly raised, angular edge and a visible depression or cutout along one side. The surface is dotted with lighter and darker speckles, suggesting a mix of fine and coarse grains. The patch appears relatively flat except for the pronounced notch, which creates a step-like drop and exposes a darker, shadowed interior. The edges are mostly straight but irregular in one area, giving the impression of a naturally eroded or partially excavated section. The coloration is consistent with urban sand or dirt, possibly found in construction zones or roadside environments. There are no visible objects or debris embedded in the sand, and the surface texture appears matte and slightly rough.
description: A flat, brown sand patch with a notched, angular edge and speckled surface, resembling compacted urban sand or dirt.
description_view_0: The sand patch is seen from above with the prominent notch and exposed darker interior clearly visible on the right side.
description_view_1: The notch is now on the left, showing the depth of the cutout and the shadowed area beneath the surface.
description_view_2: The patch appears mostly flat with the notch visible at the far left, emphasizing the smoothness of the main surface.
description_view_3: The notch is at the far right, with the main surface dominating the view and the angular edge less pronounced.
attribute_location: construction site or roadside urban area
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are mostly well-defined with one irregular, angular edge and a distinct notch, creating a clear separation from adjacent surfaces.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: The sand patch appears partially blended with its surroundings, with one edge sharply cut and the rest merging more gradually.
